message.content:
We're building on an existing Next.js app called Compass AI, an AI governance assessment tool. The main component is app/CompassApp.tsx, the API route is app/api/analyze/route.ts, and the layout is app/layout.tsx. The app uses Playfair Display and Inter fonts, a navy/gold color scheme (--navy-deep: #0d1426, --gold: #c4a55a), and produces a governance profile with sections: Overview, Frameworks, Priorities, Immediate Actions, Jurisdictional Note, and a consultant CTA.
Make the following changes:
1. Accordion results layout
Wrap each results section (Overview, Frameworks, Strategic Priorities, Immediate Actions, Jurisdictional Note) in a collapsible accordion. Overview should be open by default; all others closed. Each accordion row has a gold section label on the left, a chevron on the right that rotates on open/close, and a subtle animated expand. Do not paginate — all sections should be visible on the page simultaneously, just collapsed.
2. PDF print layout
Replace the current window.print() approach with a dedicated print stylesheet that renders a clean multi-section document. The print view should show: a branded header with the logo and consultant info (Ryan Vasquez, vasquezryanj@gmail.com), then each accordion section fully expanded as a clearly delineated block with section headers, a light divider, and readable body copy. Navy backgrounds become white, gold accents become #a8893f. No buttons, spinners, or interactive elements should appear in print. Page breaks should fall between major sections, not mid-content.
3. Loading animation
Replace the plain spinner with a branded loading sequence. Show the Compass AI logo centered, then three cycling lines of text that rotate every 1.5 seconds — things like "Mapping your jurisdictional exposure...", "Identifying applicable frameworks...", "Assessing governance maturity...", "Calculating your risk profile...". Add a subtle pulsing gold ring around the logo or an animated arc below it. Keep it refined, not playful.
4. Legal disclaimer
Directly below the risk tier badge on the results page, add a slim disclaimer bar that reads: "This profile is generated for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. Consult a qualified attorney or compliance professional before making governance decisions." Style it as a muted, small-text notice — not a warning banner. Use --text-dim color, italic, with a subtle left border in gold at low opacity.
5. Jurisdictional note — structured breakdown
Update the AI prompt in app/api/analyze/route.ts to return jurisdictionalNote as an array of objects instead of a plain string: [{ jurisdiction: string, note: string }]. Render each as a small labeled block — jurisdiction name in gold uppercase, note text below it — separated by thin dividers. If only one jurisdiction applies, it can still be an array of one.
6. Terms of use and privacy policy
Create two new pages: app/terms/page.tsx and app/privacy/page.tsx. Style them to match the app (navy background, card layout, Inter font, gold accents). Terms should cover: no legal advice, no warranty on results, no data retention (assessment answers are not stored), user responsibility. Privacy should cover: what data is collected (assessment responses sent to Anthropic API, no PII stored, no analytics), how it's used, and contact info. Add a small footer to the results page and intro page with links to both: "Terms of Use · Privacy Policy" in --text-dim style.
7. Meta tags and OG image
In app/layout.tsx, add complete Open Graph and Twitter card meta tags: title "Compass AI — AI Governance Assessment", description "Understand your AI governance obligations. Get a personalized profile mapping applicable regulations, frameworks, and next steps.", og:url set to the production domain https://compass-ai-eight.vercel.app, og:type "website". Create a static OG image at public/og-image.png — a 1200x630 dark navy rectangle with the Compass AI logo centered, the tagline below it in gold, and the URL in small text at the bottom. Generate this with a script using Pillow (Python) and commit the PNG. Reference it in the meta tags.

content.text:
Pushed. Here's what changed:

- **Each accordion section starts on its own page** — `break-before: page` (+ the legacy `page-break-before: always` for older browsers) is applied to every `.accordion-row` that follows another, and to the Consultant CTA section.
- **Content never splits mid-item** — `break-inside: avoid` is set on individual framework cards, priority items, action items, and jurisdiction blocks. If a section has too many items to fit on one page, it will overflow but each individual entry will stay intact.
- **Condensed print spacing** — Padding on cards, gaps between list items, and line-heights are all tightened for print, giving each section the best chance of fitting on a single page before the browser is forced to overflow.

Your organization operates in a high-risk regulatory environment, deploying AI systems that process personal health data and make consequential decisions affecting individuals. Applicable Frameworks and Regulations
High

Your AI systems process protected health information. HIPAA requires you to implement administrative, physical, and technical safeguards, conduct risk assessments, and maintain policies governing how PHI is used in automated systems.

NIST AI RMF
As a U.S.-based organization with high-impact AI use cases, the NIST AI Risk Management Framework provides the most practical structure for building a governance program, covering how to identify, assess, and manage AI risks across your operations.
Medium
GDPR
If any individuals whose data you process are located in the EU or UK, GDPR applies. Its Article 22 requirements around automated decision-making are directly relevant to your AI use cases.

If your AI tools assist in clinical decision-making or qualify as software as a medical device, FDA guidance on AI/ML-enabled medical devices requires pre-market review and ongoing monitoring obligations.
